source_title: #722 - 15 Lessons From 2023 - Jordan Peterson, Alex Hormozi & Elon Musk

paragraphs.speaker: chris
paragraphs.name: Chris Williamx
paragraphs.text: Hello everybody! Welcome back to the show. It is an end of 2023 review. It's the best lessons that I've learned over the last 12 months. If you want to do an end of your review, I have a free template which you can go and get at chriswillx.com slash review. It's completely free.

paragraphs.speaker: chris
paragraphs.name: Chris Williamx
paragraphs.text: First one is you should just be yourself, not because it will make you more likable, it won't, but because it's only by being yourself that you'll find people who like you for who you really are rather than someone you're pretending to be. That's from Gwynda Bogle. The advantage of doing this thing where you push off who you really are is that no criticism will ever fully land because you're one degree removed from the person who's being criticized. The disadvantage is that you're also removed from the person who's being complimented. Aubrey Marcus said, the persona is incapable of receiving love, it can only receive praise. And this is how you can feel alone in a crowd and hollow in victory. Naval Ravikant says, no one can beat you at being you. The absolute best that you can hope for if you're playing a role is to be the second best in the world at being someone else.

paragraphs.speaker: chris
paragraphs.name: Chris Williamx
paragraphs.text: Next one, it's one thing to get what you want, but it's another thing to want what's worth getting. That's from Shane Parrish. The danger of not spending time working out what you want to want can cause you, no matter how hard you work, to move in the wrong direction. Your life should be lived by design, not by default. Kyle Eschenroder said, blindly following your desires makes you a slave to your impulses, a slave to the assumptions of those around you, the advertisements you're exposed to, and the confused chemical signals of your body. In short, your default factory settings are shit. Don't follow them.

paragraphs.speaker: chris
paragraphs.name: Chris Williamx
paragraphs.text: Toxic compassion. Toxic compassion is the prioritization of short-term emotional comfort over everything else, over truth, reality, actual long-term outcomes, flourishing, everything. It optimizes for looking good rather than doing good. People would rather claim that body fat has no bearing on health and mortality outcomes to avoid making overweight people feel upset, even if this causes them to literally die sooner or have a worse quality of life over the long run. Elon Musk said, what I care about is the reality of goodness, not the perception of it. And what I see all over the place is people who care about looking good while doing evil. The important trade-off is between appearing good and actually doing good.

paragraphs.speaker: chris
paragraphs.name: Chris Williamx
paragraphs.text: Elon on Lex's show. Lex asked how he was doing and what it's like to be him. And Elon replied, my mind is a storm. I don't think most people would want to be me. They may think they would want to be me, but they don't. They don't know. They don't understand. And this is why jealousy is a stupid emotion. Naval says about jealousy, I realized that all of these people I was jealous of, I couldn't just cherry pick and choose little aspects of their life. You have to be that person. You actually want to be that person with all of their reactions, their desires, their family, their happiness level, their outlook on life, their self-image. If you're not willing to do a wholesale, 24-7, 100% swap with who that person is, then there is no point in being jealous.

paragraphs.speaker: chris
paragraphs.name: Chris Williamx
paragraphs.text: Jimmy Carr has this really wonderful idea about the difference between trajectory and position and how important trajectory is. If you are number two in the world, but last year you were number one, that is way worse than sitting at number 150, but being on this huge upward slope from being number 30 twelve months ago. Ryan Long said, there's an old saying that there's three types of people on a ladder, one at the bottom, one at the middle, and one at the top. Which one is the best to be? The one that's still climbing. Andrew Tate, having things isn't fun, getting things is fun. Any accomplishment is just a new higher bar for you to get over in future.

paragraphs.speaker: chris
paragraphs.name: Chris Williamx
paragraphs.text: Robert Sapolsky, I haven't been able to stop thinking about it: Dopamine is not about the pursuit of happiness. It is about the happiness of pursuit. So much of life and enjoyment is about the anticipation of things coming. In fact, the anticipation is often actually more enjoyable than the experience.
